phase-plan.purpose: Define how AI integration features are gated, released, and accelerated so we can ship quickly while preserving rollback safety and clean architecture boundaries.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: server-controlled feature flags, rollout cohorts, lane-based execution strategy (first-party, hybrid third-party, managed third-party), and exit criteria. Out of scope: vendor contracts, billing implementation, and final provider lock-in decision.

file.description: Track Phase 50 planning slices and rollout strategy references.
Assign 50-02 ownership and add lane decision task.
Record flag-first rollout policy and acceleration-lane policy.
Register active agent and phase pointer for this execution slice.

questions.question: Where should flag evaluation live first: existing app server runtime config, or a dedicated flag service?
Do we commit to lane B (hybrid third-party) as default fast path unless blocked by compliance or cost?

Lane switch thresholds: what numeric values trigger switching away from the current acceleration lane? (e.g. delivery slip %, incident rate, cost per active user—the “exit criteria” for the current lane.)

task.description: Define flag map and rollout cohorts for all new AI integration features.
Define lane scorecard with speed/risk/lock-in metrics and switching criteria.
Bind 50-03/50-04/50-05 tasks to selected lane and flags.

decision.summary: Ship all new AI capabilities behind server-side flags with kill-switches and cohort rollouts.
decision.impact: Supports fast deployment and immediate rollback without reverting merges.

decision.summary: Use three execution lanes and choose default lane by scorecard, not preference.
decision.impact: Maintains speed while preventing accidental long-term lock-in.

flag-map.flag: Controls Assistant UI -> app server routed sessions.
Controls write access to content-model schema mutation endpoints.
Controls third-party provider path in hybrid/managed lanes.

rollout-cohorts.cohort: pilot: selected users; throttled write operations
general: broad rollout with guarded defaults

lane.name: First-party fast path
lane.notes: Use existing app server + MCP + in-repo storage/contracts only.

lane.name: Hybrid third-party
lane.notes: Keep core orchestration in app server; optionally use third-party for auth/session/observability acceleration.

lane.name: Managed third-party heavy
lane.notes: Max speed using managed components; accept stronger vendor coupling.
